 {"id":"bd-1olr","title":"Document filter mode in TUI help overlay","description":"# Document Filter Mode in TUI Help

## Problem
The TUI has a filter mode (activated by /) that is completely undocumented. In widgets.rs line 109:
```rust
if state.filter_mode {
    render_filter_input(frame, state, &colors);
}
```

Users discover this by accident. The help overlay (?) doesn't mention it.

## Current Help Overlay Content
From render_help_overlay:
```
q/Esc   Quit
↑/↓     Navigate
Tab     Switch panel
r       Refresh
?       Toggle help
```

## Updated Help Content
```
Navigation
  ↑/↓ j/k   Navigate up/down
  Tab       Next panel
  Shift+Tab Previous panel
  1-4       Jump to panel

Actions
  Enter     Select/expand
  r         Refresh data
  c         Copy to clipboard
  d         Drain worker (Workers panel)
  e         Enable worker (Workers panel)
  k         Cancel build (Builds panel)

Search
  /         Open filter (Build History)
  Esc       Close filter
  Enter     Apply filter

General
  ?         Toggle this help
  q/Esc     Quit
```

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (render_help_overlay function)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:04:17.153326791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:20.892931011Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:19:20.892861572Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1olr","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1olr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/tui/widgets_test.rs)
1. `test_help_overlay_contains_filter_shortcut()` - Verify / is documented
2. `test_help_overlay_contains_all_shortcuts()` - Verify completeness
3. `test_help_overlay_section_ordering()` - Verify logical grouping
4. `test_help_overlay_fits_80x24()` - Verify renders in minimal terminal

### Integration Tests
1. Render help overlay and verify all keybindings are documented
2. Press each documented key and verify action occurs
3. Verify no undocumented keybindings exist

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_tui_help.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_tui_help_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing TUI help overlay completeness"

# Dump help overlay content
HELP_OUTPUT=$(rch dashboard --test-mode --show-help --dump-state 2>&1)

# Required shortcuts that must be documented
REQUIRED_SHORTCUTS=(
  "/" "filter" "search"
  "j" "k" "navigation" "up" "down"
  "d" "drain"
  "e" "enable"
  "q" "quit"
  "?" "help"
  "Tab" "panel"
)

for shortcut in "${REQUIRED_SHORTCUTS[@]}"; do
  if ! echo "$HELP_OUTPUT" | grep -qi "$shortcut"; then
    log "FAIL: Missing documentation for: $shortcut"
    exit 1
  fi
  log "PASS: Found documentation for: $shortcut"
done

log "All help overlay tests passed"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log help overlay render at DEBUG level
- Log any keybinding conflicts at WARN level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:55:35Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1q0e","title":"WS4.3: Add comprehensive tests for multi-command classification","description":"Comprehensive test suite for multi-command classification (WS4):

1. SPLIT_COMMANDS UNIT TESTS:

   Basic splitting:
   - "cargo build" → ["cargo build"]
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → ["cargo fmt", "cargo build"]
   - "cd /proj && cmake .. && make" → ["cd /proj", "cmake ..", "make"]
   - "make || echo fail" → ["make", "echo fail"]
   - "cd /tmp && make -j4 || echo fail" → ["cd /tmp", "make -j4", "echo fail"]
   - "a ; b ; c" → ["a", "b", "c"]
   - " && cargo build" → ["", "cargo build"] or ["cargo build"] (decide behavior)
   - "cargo build --release" → ["cargo build --release"]

2. QUOTING EDGE CASES:

   Single quotes:
   - "echo '&&' && cargo build" → ["echo '&&'", "cargo build"]
   - "echo 'hello;world' && make" → ["echo 'hello;world'", "make"]

   Double quotes:
   - "echo \"&&\" && cargo build" → ["echo \"&&\"", "cargo build"]
   - "echo \"it's && done\" && make" → ["echo \"it's && done\"", "make"]

   Backticks (legacy command substitution):
   - "echo \`date\` && cargo build" → ["echo \`date\`", "cargo build"]
   - Document if backtick-nested && is handled or not

   Escaped quotes:
   - "echo \\\"hello && cargo build" → behavior depends on shell, document choice

   ANSI-C quoting ($'...'):
   - "$'hello\\nworld' && cargo build" — document if supported

3. CLASSIFICATION INTEGRATION TESTS:

   Should classify as COMPILATION:
   - "cargo fmt && cargo build" → is_compilation: true
   - "cd /project && make -j8" → is_compilation: true
   - "export RUSTFLAGS='-C opt-level=3' && cargo build --release" → is_compilation: true
   - "mkdir -p build && cmake -B build && cmake --build build" → is_compilation: true
   - "echo 'Starting...' && cargo test" → is_compilation: true (second segment)

   Should classify as NON-COMPILATION:
   - "echo hello && echo world" → is_compilation: false
   - "ls -la && cat file.txt" → is_compilation: false
   - "git status && git log" → is_compilation: false

4. FAIL-OPEN EDGE CASES:

   Malformed input (should NOT panic, return usable result):
   - Unclosed single quote: "echo 'hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed double quote: "echo \"hello && cargo build"
   - Unclosed backtick: "echo \`date && cargo build"
   - Empty string: ""
   - Only whitespace: "   "
   - Very long input (10KB+): verify no timeout/panic
   - Input with null bytes: "cargo\x00build" (if applicable)
   - Unicode: "echo '你好' && cargo build"

5. REGRESSION TESTS:
   - All existing classification tests must still pass
   - Single-command classification behavior unchanged
   - Performance: classify_command("cargo fmt && cargo build") completes in <5ms

6. PIPE PRESERVATION:
   - "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" → single segment (pipe is NOT a command separator)
   - "make 2>&1 | grep error && echo done" → ["make 2>&1 | grep error", "echo done"]

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (#[cfg(test)] module)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:09.996516181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:24:57.694130987Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-16t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-1qt9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q0e","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1q3p","title":"Idea: Soft-fail artifact retrieval with actionable diff","description":"## Background
Artifact retrieval can fail even if remote build succeeded (rsync issues, permissions). Hard failure can confuse users if build output already streamed. A softer error with actionable detail improves UX.

## Goals
- Detect artifact retrieval failures and present clear remediation.
- Keep fail-open semantics for subsequent commands.

## Design / Approach
- On artifact retrieval failure, log which patterns were attempted and rsync stderr snippet.
- Return a warning and allow continuation (no denial change for already executed commands).
- Provide `rch diagnose` suggestion to re-run with verbose or fetch artifacts manually.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Enhance error classification for retrieve_artifacts.
- Add warning display + JSON fields for automation.
- Update docs/troubleshooting.

## Tests
- Unit: error classification includes attempted patterns.
- Integration: mock retrieval failure yields warning (not panic).
- E2E: warning displayed in verbose mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Retrieval failure is reported clearly without crashing.
- User sees actionable next steps.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Remote success but artifact retrieval fails -> warn but keep deny.
- Include attempted patterns + rsync stderr snippet.
- Provide actionable follow-up steps in message.

## E2E Outline
- Mock retrieval failure -> warning emitted with patterns.
- Exit code unchanged from remote success path.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: artifact retrieval failure classification.
- rch/src/hook.rs: warning path does not alter deny/allow semantics.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh: mock retrieval failure -> warning + actionable info.
","notes":"## Implementation Complete (EmeraldMoose)

### Changes Made:
1. **Added ArtifactRetrievalWarning struct** (rch/src/error.rs)
   - Captures attempted patterns, rsync stderr snippet, exit code
   - Includes actionable suggestions
   - Provides format_warning() for human-readable output
   - Provides to_json() for machine-readable output
   - Implements Display trait

2. **Updated artifact retrieval error handling** (rch/src/hook.rs)
   - Extracts rsync exit code from error message
   - Creates structured ArtifactRetrievalWarning on failure
   - Shows detailed warning in verbose/interactive mode
   - Outputs JSON in machine mode for automation
   - Maintains soft-fail semantics (compilation still succeeds)

3. **Added 6 unit tests** for ArtifactRetrievalWarning:
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_basic
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_format
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_json
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_truncates_stderr
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_with_custom_suggestions
   - test_artifact_retrieval_warning_display_trait

### Remaining work:
- E2E test script (scripts/e2e_bd-1q3p.sh) - optional
- Update docs/troubleshooting - optional

### Verification:
- cargo check passes
- All 6 new tests pass
- 1099 total tests in rch package pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:55:32.592525740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635332867Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:12:05.635214798Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1qfm","title":"Add code coverage reporting (cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov)","description":"**Add code coverage reporting with cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov**

## Scope
Integrate code coverage tooling into the CI pipeline:
- Choose between cargo-tarpaulin (easier) or llvm-cov (more accurate)
- Generate coverage reports in multiple formats (HTML, LCOV, JSON)
- Upload to coverage service (Codecov or Coveralls)

## Requirements
- Coverage for all crates (rch, rchd, rch-common, rch-wkr, rch-telemetry)
- Exclude test code from coverage metrics
- Generate per-file and per-function coverage
- Integration with GitHub Actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Coverage reports generated on every CI run
- [ ] HTML report viewable as artifact
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-crate coverage breakdown
- [ ] Historical coverage tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteDune","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:03:31.044005621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481769932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:10.481689001Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria verified: CI generates LCOV/HTML reports, Codecov badge in README, per-crate summary via coverage-summary.md, test code excluded via .cargo/config.toml","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qfm","depends_on_id":"bd-2mc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qt9","title":"WS4.2: Integrate sub-command classification into classify_command()","description":"Modify the classification flow to handle multi-command strings:

Option A (recommended): Modify classify_command() at line 243:
  1. Call split_shell_commands(cmd)
  2. If only 1 segment → proceed as before (no behavior change)
  3. If multiple segments → classify each through the full pipeline
  4. Return the "most significant" result:
     - If ANY segment is compilation → return compilation (with highest confidence)
     - If ALL segments are non-compilation → return non-compilation

Option B: Modify check_structure() to skip &&/||/; rejection and instead return a "multi-command" indicator that the caller handles.

Option A is cleaner because it keeps the split logic at the top level.

IMPORTANT: The original command string is passed to the worker unchanged. We only split for classification purposes. The worker executes the full "cargo fmt && cargo build" as-is.

RECURSIVE CLASSIFICATION: When classifying sub-commands, use the same classify_command() function. This means sub-commands go through all 5 tiers. Guard against infinite recursion by passing a depth parameter or a flag that prevents re-splitting.

File: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (classify_command at line 243, check_structure at line 515)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:08.232803684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:08:14.733082845Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:08:14.733013386Z","close_reason":"Integrated multi-command splitting into classify_command(). Added classify_command_inner with depth guard (MAX_CLASSIFY_DEPTH=1) to prevent infinite recursion. Multi-command strings are split via split_shell_commands, each sub-command classified through full 5-tier pipeline, most significant compilation result returned. Updated 8 tests across patterns.rs, patterns_security_test.rs, proptest_tests.rs, e2e_hook.rs to reflect new behavior. All tests pass, clippy clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-2myq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qt9","depends_on_id":"bd-9ka6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ib0","title":"Unify worker state terminology across CLI","description":"# Unify Worker State Terminology

## Problem
The CLI uses confusing, overlapping terms for worker states:

### Current Commands
- `rch workers drain css` - Stops sending new jobs, waits for current to finish
- `rch workers disable css` - Also stops sending jobs? How different from drain?
- `rch workers enable css` - Resumes sending jobs

### User Confusion
Users don't understand:
1. What's the difference between drain and disable?
2. Is 'paused' the same as 'drained'?
3. Can a disabled worker be drained?
4. What's the opposite of drain - 'undrain' or 'enable'?

## Proposed Terminology

### Worker States (clear hierarchy)
```
ENABLED (normal operation)
  ↓ rch workers drain
DRAINING (accepting no new jobs, finishing current)
  ↓ (all jobs complete)
DRAINED (idle, no jobs, ready to disable)
  ↓ rch workers disable
DISABLED (completely offline, skipped by selection)
```

### Commands
```
rch workers drain <id>     Start graceful drain
rch workers enable <id>    Resume from drained/draining
rch workers disable <id>   Mark worker as offline (must be drained first)
rch workers status <id>    Show detailed worker state
```

### Remove/Deprecate
- Remove 'pause' terminology entirely
- Deprecate direct 'disable' without drain (or require --force)

## Documentation Updates
- Update --help text for all worker commands
- Add state diagram to rch workers --help
- Update README.md worker management section

## Migration
1. Add deprecation warning to confusing usages
2. Keep old behavior for 1-2 releases
3. Remove deprecated paths

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/main.rs (command definitions, help text)
- rch/src/commands.rs (worker state handling)
- rch/src/status_display.rs (status output)
- README.md (documentation)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:06:08.311137466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:46:46.819516487Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:46:46.819450474Z","close_reason":"Added Drained state to WorkerStatus enum with automatic transition from Draining when all jobs complete. Updated theme, UI, API, health, alerts, telemetry, workers, and selection to handle new state. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3jru","title":"Implement MetricsDashboard for periodic stats display","description":"Create MetricsDashboard in rchd/src/ui/metrics.rs for performance summary:
- Periodic statistics panel (configurable interval, default 5min)
- Jobs completed, failed, cache hits in period
- Average job duration with trend indicator (↑↓→)
- Worker utilization percentages
- Transfer volume (bytes synced up/down)

Technical requirements:
- Only display when stderr is TTY and rich output enabled
- Use Table for metrics with right-aligned numbers
- Include sparkline-style trend indicators using Unicode blocks
- Calculate and display percentiles (p50, p95, p99) for latency
- Reset counters option with --metrics-reset-interval
- Support Prometheus-compatible metric names in description

Example periodic output:
╭─ Metrics (last 5 minutes) ───────────────────╮
│ Jobs: 47 completed, 2 failed (96% success)   │
│ Cache: 38 hits, 9 misses (81% hit rate)      │
│ Avg Duration: 23.4s (↓ improving)            │
│ Workers: 89% utilized │ Queue peak: 12 jobs  │
│ Transfer: ↑ 1.2 GB  ↓ 3.4 GB                 │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────╯","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:56.185396572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:04.682633418Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:46:04.682566413Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jru","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jsw","title":"WS1.5: (Optional) Add in-memory cache for TimingHistory","description":"FOLLOW-UP OPTIMIZATION after WS1.1-WS1.4 are complete.

PROBLEM: spawn_blocking moves I/O off the async thread but doesn't reduce latency. If the timing history file is slow to read (HDD, cold cache, large file), the decision path can still exceed the 5ms budget.

SOLUTION: Keep TimingHistory in memory with write-through to disk.

Implementation:
1. Add a static or process-global cache: OnceCell<RwLock<TimingHistory>>
2. On first access (lazy load): spawn_blocking(load) and store in cache
3. estimate_timing_for_build: Read from cache (no disk I/O)
4. record_build_timing: Update cache AND spawn_blocking(save) for persistence
5. Process restart: Cache is cold, first access loads from disk

Benefits:
- Decision path has zero disk I/O after first load
- Writes are async and don't block decisions
- Survives process restart (data persisted to disk)

Risks:
- Cache stale if file is edited externally (acceptable for this use case)
- Memory usage (timing history is small, ~KB)

This is P3 because spawn_blocking in WS1.1-WS1.2 is sufficient for most cases. This optimization is for high-throughput scenarios.

 {"id":"bd-3knb","title":"Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml","description":"# Add rich_rust dependency to workspace Cargo.toml

## Task Description

Add rich_rust as a workspace dependency in the root Cargo.toml, then add it to each relevant crate's Cargo.toml (rch, rchd, rch-common).

## Background

rich_rust is located at /dp/rich_rust during development. It provides:
- Console struct for terminal output
- Style and Color types for styling
- Table, Panel, Tree, ProgressBar renderables
- Markup parsing ([bold red]text[/])
- Automatic terminal capability detection

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Add to workspace Cargo.toml

```toml
[workspace.dependencies]
# During development, use path:
rich_rust = { path = "/dp/rich_rust", features = ["full"] }

# When published, switch to:
# rich_rust = { version = "0.1", features = ["full"] }
```

### 2. Add to rch/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

### 3. Add to rchd/Cargo.toml

Same as rch/Cargo.toml.

### 4. Add to rch-common/Cargo.toml

```toml
[dependencies]
# Minimal features for shared types
rich_rust = { workspace = true, optional = true, default-features = false }

[features]
default = ["rich-ui"]
rich-ui = ["rich_rust"]
```

## Technical Notes

### Why "full" features?
The full feature set includes:
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON pretty-printing (serde_json)

We want all of these for maximum capability.

### Why optional?
Making rich_rust optional allows:
1. Compile-time removal if issues arise
2. Smaller binaries for users who dont need rich output
3. Faster compilation without feature

### Path vs Published
During development, use path dependency for rapid iteration.
Switch to crates.io version when rich_rust is published.

## Verification

```bash
# Should compile successfully
cargo check --all-targets

# Should compile without rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --no-default-features

# Should compile with rich-ui
cargo check --all-targets --features rich-ui
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] Workspace Cargo.toml has rich_rust in [workspace.dependencies]
2. [ ] rch/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
3. [ ] rchd/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with optional + feature flag
4. [ ] rch-common/Cargo.toml has rich_rust with minimal features
5. [ ] cargo check passes with default features
6. [ ] cargo check passes with --no-default-features","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:51.267049982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865319328Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:33:17.865271618Z","close_reason":"Added rich_rust as workspace dependency with optional feature flags in rch, rchd, and rch-common. Fixed unicode-width version conflict and clippy warnings in selection.rs.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3knb","depends_on_id":"bd-38kz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3mog","title":"WS2.4: Unit tests for zero-allocation reject path","description":"Add tests that verify zero heap allocations on the reject path:

1. Classification correctness tests:
   - Test all existing test cases still pass after Cow conversion
   - Specifically test that known non-compilation commands (ls, cat, echo, git status) produce correct Classification with Cow::Borrowed reason
   - Test that compilation commands (cargo build, make, gcc) produce correct Classification

2. Allocation-free reject path verification:
   - Use a custom allocator counter (or #[global_allocator] with counting) to verify that classify_command("ls") produces zero heap allocations
   - Alternative: use std::hint::black_box + criterion benchmark showing 0 allocs
   - At minimum: verify the reason field is Cow::Borrowed (not Cow::Owned) for reject cases

3. Cow roundtrip tests:
   - Verify Classification.reason can be displayed, compared, serialized/deserialized
   - Verify ClassificationTier.name and .reason work with serde (Cow<str> serializes as string)

Test location: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (inline #[cfg(test)] module)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:52:50.341239385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:53:07.233080728Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mog","depends_on_id":"bd-25cj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mog","depends_on_id":"bd-3uo1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mog","depends_on_id":"bd-54u8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3n00","title":"Create Unit Tests for Test Harness & Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the test harness itself, ensuring the test infrastructure is reliable before using it to test rch.

## Why This Matters
- A buggy test harness produces false positives/negatives
- Harness bugs are harder to debug than rch bugs
- Unit tests catch harness issues before they affect e2e tests
- Provides documentation of expected harness behavior

## Test Categories

### 1. Logging Infrastructure Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/logging_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_log_entry_is_valid_json() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("test_example");
    logger.info("test message", &[("key", "value")]);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    for line in log_content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).expect("invalid JSON");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_timing_accuracy() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_log_levels_filter_correctly() { ... }
```

### 2. Output Comparison Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_identical_outputs_compare_equal() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization() {
    let local = "/home/user/project/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let remote = "/tmp/rch/project_abc/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let normalizer = OutputNormalizer::default();
    assert_eq!(normalizer.normalize(local), normalizer.normalize(remote));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_shows_actual_differences() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_exact() { ... }
```

### 3. Worker Discovery Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/discovery_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_workers_available_returns_false_when_no_config() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_skip_mechanism_logs_reason() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_health_check_timeout() { ... }
```

### 4. Harness Lifecycle Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/harness_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_daemon_spawn_and_cleanup() {
    let harness = TrueE2EHarness::new_or_skip().unwrap();
    // Daemon should be running
    assert!(harness.daemon_is_healthy());
    drop(harness);
    // Daemon should be cleaned up (if harness spawned it)
}

#[test]
fn test_fixture_isolation() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_project_cleanup() { ... }
```

### 5. Timing Utilities Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/timing_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_measure_command_timing_accuracy() {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let measured = measure_command_timing("sleep 0.1");
    let actual = start.elapsed();
    // Should be within 10ms of actual
    assert!((measured.as_millis() as i64 - actual.as_millis() as i64).abs() < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_percentile_calculation() { ... }
```

## Test Organization
```
tests/
└── true_e2e/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── harness.rs
    ├── logging.rs
    ├── output.rs
    └── tests/           # Unit tests for harness
        ├── mod.rs
        ├── logging_tests.rs
        ├── output_tests.rs
        ├── discovery_tests.rs
        ├── harness_tests.rs
        └── timing_tests.rs
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% code coverage for logging module
- [ ] All output comparison edge cases tested
- [ ] Worker discovery tests run without real workers
- [ ] Harness lifecycle tested end-to-end
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test` (no workers needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:54.582781639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.357344236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.356337229Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-1tq2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3r2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8l6b","title":"Create Test Worker Configuration (workers_test.toml)","description":"## Purpose
Create a test-only worker configuration that points to real, available SSH-accessible worker machines for true e2e testing.

## Requirements
1. `tests/e2e/workers_test.toml` file with at least 1 real worker
2. Worker must have: passwordless SSH, Rust nightly, rsync, zstd
3. Validate worker is actually reachable via SSH
4. Document worker requirements for CI setup

## Configuration Format
```toml
# tests/e2e/workers_test.toml

[workers.css]
hostname = "css.example.com"
user = "builder"
port = 22
identity_file = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519"
remote_work_dir = "/tmp/rch_test"

# Optional: multiple workers for load distribution
[workers.gpu2]
hostname = "gpu2.example.com"
user = "builder"
remote_work_dir = "/data/rch_test"

[settings]
default_timeout_seconds = 300
ssh_connection_timeout = 10
rsync_compression = "zstd"
```

## Implementation Notes
- Can use the same workers as production (css, gpu2, etc)
- Should include health check to skip tests if workers unavailable
- Consider env var `RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG` to override

## Unit Tests for Config Validation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/config_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_config_parses_valid_toml() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(config.workers.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(config.workers["test"].hostname, "test.example.com");
}

#[test]
fn test_config_rejects_missing_hostname() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let result: Result<WorkerConfig, _> = toml::from_str(config_str);
    assert!(result.is_err());
}

#[test]
fn test_config_default_port() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(config.workers["test"].port.unwrap_or(22), 22);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_expands_home_in_identity_file() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [workers.test]
        hostname = "test.example.com"
        user = "builder"
        identity_file = "~/.ssh/id_ed25519"
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    let expanded = expand_path(&config.workers["test"].identity_file.as_ref().unwrap());
    assert!(!expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("~"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_from_env_override() {
    env::set_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG", "/custom/path.toml");
    let path = get_config_path();
    assert_eq!(path, PathBuf::from("/custom/path.toml"));
    env::remove_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG");
}

#[test]
fn test_config_missing_file_returns_error() {
    let result = load_worker_config("/nonexistent/workers_test.toml");
    assert!(result.is_err());
    assert!(result.unwrap_err().to_string().contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_empty_workers_is_valid_but_useless() {
    let config_str = r#"
        [settings]
        default_timeout_seconds = 300
    "#;
    let config: WorkerConfig = toml::from_str(config_str).unwrap();
    assert!(config.workers.is_empty());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config file exists and is valid TOML
- [ ] At least one worker can be reached via SSH
- [ ] Worker has required toolchain installed
- [ ] Unit tests for config validation pass
- [ ] Config handles missing/invalid gracefully with clear errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:55.353755779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:48:37.417413247Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:48:37.417350309Z","close_reason":"Created test worker configuration infrastructure: workers_test.toml template and rch-common/src/e2e/test_workers.rs module with 26 passing tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8l6b","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9eml","title":"Unit Tests: Fleet Management Module (No Mocks)","description":"# Unit Tests: Fleet Management Module (No Mocks)

## Target Files

All in `rch/src/fleet/`:
- plan.rs - Fleet planning logic
- executor.rs - Fleet execution engine  
- preflight.rs - Pre-flight checks
- dry_run.rs - Dry-run simulation
- history.rs - History tracking
- rollback.rs - Rollback functionality
- audit.rs - Audit trails

## Test Strategy

### Test Design (No Mocks)

1. **Use TempDir**: All file operations use real temp directories
2. **Use Localhost**: Command execution tests run real commands locally
3. **Use Real Files**: Cargo.toml, src/main.rs are actual files
4. **Test Real Behavior**: Verify actual output, not mocked responses

### Required Tests Per File

| File | Tests |
|------|-------|
| plan.rs | plan_single_worker, plan_multi_priority, plan_empty_error |
| executor.rs | execute_real_command, capture_stderr, respect_timeout |
| preflight.rs | check_real_paths, detect_missing_cargo, validate_toolchain |
| dry_run.rs | dry_run_logs_only, dry_run_no_side_effects |
| history.rs | persist_to_file, load_from_file, append_entries |
| rollback.rs | create_checkpoint, restore_checkpoint, cleanup_temp |
| audit.rs | log_event, persist_audit_trail, query_by_date |

### Test Template

```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_single_worker() {
    // Structured logging
    tracing::info!("TEST START: plan_single_worker");
    
    // Real temp directory
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    
    // Real operation
    let plan = FleetPlan::new(&workers, tmp.path());
    
    // Real assertion
    assert_eq!(plan.workers().len(), 1);
    
    tracing::info!("TEST PASS: plan_single_worker");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 3+ tests per module (21 total minimum)
- [ ] All tests use real filesystem operations
- [ ] All tests have TEST START/PASS logging
- [ ] No mock usage (except for external SSH)
- [ ] `cargo test fleet` passes","notes":"Verified comprehensive test coverage:

**Tests per module (requirement: 3+):**
- audit: 24 tests ✅
- dry_run: 31 tests ✅
- executor: 8 tests ✅
- history: 14 tests ✅
- plan: 31 tests ✅
- preflight: 16 tests ✅
- rollback: 7 tests ✅

**Total: 131 fleet tests (requirement: 21 minimum) ✅**

All tests pass. No mocks detected in test code.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildSpring","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:57:51.268727446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:15:34.862252991Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:15:34.862181978Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9eml","depends_on_id":"bd-1aim","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-9ka6","title":"WS4: Smart Multi-Command Classification","description":"PROBLEM: rch-common/src/patterns.rs check_structure() function (line 515) blanket-rejects commands containing:
  - "&&" (line 547): "chained command (&&)"
  - "||" (line 550): "chained command (||)"
  - ";" (line 544): "chained command (;)"

This is in Tier 2 (structure_analysis), NOT Tier 1 as originally stated. All three operators cause immediate rejection before any keyword/classification analysis runs.

WHY IT MATTERS: AI coding agents commonly chain commands: "cargo fmt && cargo build", "cd project && make", "export VAR=val && cargo build --release". Blanket rejection means these compilable chains are never offloaded.

SOLUTION: Instead of rejecting multi-command strings, split on unquoted &&, ||, ; operators and classify each sub-command independently. If ANY sub-command classifies as compilation, the overall result is "compilable". The original unsplit command string is sent to the worker.

INTEGRATION POINT: check_structure() at line 515. The split-and-reclassify logic should be added to classify_command() / classify_command_detailed() BEFORE check_structure() is called, or check_structure() should be modified to not reject these operators when the caller indicates multi-command mode.

ALSO AFFECTED: Pipe rejection (line 522) — "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" is rejected as "piped command". This is a separate issue but related. Consider as a stretch goal.

FILES: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (check_structure at 515, classify_command at 243, classify_command_detailed at 283)
RISK: High — changes core classification semantics. Needs comprehensive tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.247323586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T22:55:29.476593602Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) frequently emit chained commands:
  cargo fmt && cargo build && cargo test
  cd /project && make -j$(nproc)
  export CC=clang && cmake -B build && cmake --build build

The current Tier 1 rejection of && means NONE of these are offloaded. This is arguably the single highest-impact fix in this audit — it directly increases the fraction of compilable work that gets offloaded.

DESIGN DECISION: We classify sub-commands independently but execute the ORIGINAL command string on the worker. The hook does not rewrite commands — it only decides local vs. remote. This preserves the fail-open design: if our splitting is imperfect, the worst case is local execution (safe).

PERFORMANCE IMPACT: split_commands adds ~1-2μs to classification. But the benefit is offloading a 30-60 second compilation to a remote worker. The ROI is >1000x.

RISK MITIGATION: Comprehensive test suite (WS4.3) covers quoting edge cases. The splitter must handle single quotes, double quotes, backticks, and escaped characters correctly. A bug here could cause misclassification.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:36Z"},{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION FROM REVIEW: The && rejection is in Tier 2 (check_structure, line 547), NOT Tier 1 as originally stated. Tier 1 is instant rejection of empty/short commands. Tier 2 is structure analysis.

Also: the rejection covers THREE operators, not just &&:
  - ; at line 544
  - && at line 547
  - || at line 550
All three need to be handled by the command splitter.

RECURSIVE CLASSIFICATION GUARD: When classify_command() encounters a multi-command string, it splits and re-classifies each segment. Each segment will go through check_structure() again. If a segment contains NO operators, check_structure() won't reject it — good. But if someone writes "a && b && c", the top-level split produces ["a", "b", "c"], each of which is a single command — no recursion issue. The only risk is a command like "a && (b && c)" where the inner && is inside parens — but parens are already rejected by check_structure() line 529, so this case is already handled.","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:55:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-9ka6","title":"WS4: Smart Multi-Command Classification","description":"PROBLEM: rch-common/src/patterns.rs check_structure() function (line 515) blanket-rejects commands containing:
  - "&&" (line 547): "chained command (&&)"
  - "||" (line 550): "chained command (||)"
  - ";" (line 544): "chained command (;)"

This is in Tier 2 (structure_analysis), NOT Tier 1 as originally stated. All three operators cause immediate rejection before any keyword/classification analysis runs.

WHY IT MATTERS: AI coding agents commonly chain commands: "cargo fmt && cargo build", "cd project && make", "export VAR=val && cargo build --release". Blanket rejection means these compilable chains are never offloaded.

SOLUTION: Instead of rejecting multi-command strings, split on unquoted &&, ||, ; operators and classify each sub-command independently. If ANY sub-command classifies as compilation, the overall result is "compilable". The original unsplit command string is sent to the worker.

INTEGRATION POINT: check_structure() at line 515. The split-and-reclassify logic should be added to classify_command() / classify_command_detailed() BEFORE check_structure() is called, or check_structure() should be modified to not reject these operators when the caller indicates multi-command mode.

ALSO AFFECTED: Pipe rejection (line 522) — "cargo build 2>&1 | tee log" is rejected as "piped command". This is a separate issue but related. Consider as a stretch goal.

FILES: rch-common/src/patterns.rs (check_structure at 515, classify_command at 243, classify_command_detailed at 283)
RISK: High — changes core classification semantics. Needs comprehensive tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:36:49.247323586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:28:07.813759609Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:28:07.813667317Z","close_reason":"All children complete: WS4.1 shell command splitter (bd-2myq), WS4.2 integrate into classify_command (bd-1qt9), WS4.3 comprehensive tests (bd-1q0e), WS4.4 fail-open safeguards (bd-16t3). Multi-command classification fully implemented with 30+ tests covering splitting, integration, safeguards, performance, and edge cases.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mmt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) frequently emit chained commands:
  cargo fmt && cargo build && cargo test
  cd /project && make -j$(nproc)
  export CC=clang && cmake -B build && cmake --build build

The current Tier 1 rejection of && means NONE of these are offloaded. This is arguably the single highest-impact fix in this audit — it directly increases the fraction of compilable work that gets offloaded.

DESIGN DECISION: We classify sub-commands independently but execute the ORIGINAL command string on the worker. The hook does not rewrite commands — it only decides local vs. remote. This preserves the fail-open design: if our splitting is imperfect, the worst case is local execution (safe).

PERFORMANCE IMPACT: split_commands adds ~1-2μs to classification. But the benefit is offloading a 30-60 second compilation to a remote worker. The ROI is >1000x.

RISK MITIGATION: Comprehensive test suite (WS4.3) covers quoting edge cases. The splitter must handle single quotes, double quotes, backticks, and escaped characters correctly. A bug here could cause misclassification.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:36Z"},{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-9ka6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION FROM REVIEW: The && rejection is in Tier 2 (check_structure, line 547), NOT Tier 1 as originally stated. Tier 1 is instant rejection of empty/short commands. Tier 2 is structure analysis.

Also: the rejection covers THREE operators, not just &&:
  - ; at line 544
  - && at line 547
  - || at line 550
All three need to be handled by the command splitter.

RECURSIVE CLASSIFICATION GUARD: When classify_command() encounters a multi-command string, it splits and re-classifies each segment. Each segment will go through check_structure() again. If a segment contains NO operators, check_structure() won't reject it — good. But if someone writes "a && b && c", the top-level split produces ["a", "b", "c"], each of which is a single command — no recursion issue. The only risk is a command like "a && (b && c)" where the inner && is inside parens — but parens are already rejected by check_structure() line 529, so this case is already handled.","created_at":"2026-01-29T22:55:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9ygg","title":"Test: Project rsync Transfer to Workers","description":"## Purpose
Test that project files are correctly synced to workers via rsync with proper exclusions and compression.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_rsync_initial","phase":"sync","msg":"Starting rsync","data":{"source":"fixtures/hello_world","dest":"css:/tmp/rch_test","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_rsync_initial","phase":"sync_complete","msg":"Rsync completed","data":{"files_transferred":15,"bytes_sent":12345,"duration_ms":1234}}
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_rsync_exclusion","phase":"verify","msg":"Exclusion check","data":{"pattern":"target/","excluded":true}}
```

## Test Cases

### Basic Sync
1. Initial sync:
   - Sync hello_world fixture to worker
   - Verify: all source files present on worker
   - Verify: correct permissions
   - Log: files transferred, bytes, duration

2. Incremental sync:
   - Modify one file, sync again
   - Verify: only changed file transferred
   - Verify: rsync reports minimal transfer
   - Log: incremental vs full comparison

3. Sync with deletions:
   - Delete local file, sync
   - Verify: file removed on worker (or not, based on config)
   - Log: delete behavior

### Exclusion Patterns
4. target/ excluded:
   - Local target/ exists
   - Verify: not synced to worker
   - Log: exclusion verified

5. .git/objects excluded:
   - Verify: .git/objects not synced
   - But: .gitignore IS synced (needed for build)
   - Log: .git handling

6. node_modules excluded:
   - Verify: not synced to worker
   - Log: exclusion verified

7. Build artifacts excluded:
   - .rlib, .rmeta, .o files not synced
   - Log: artifact exclusion

### Compression
8. zstd compression:
   - Verify rsync uses compression
   - Verify: transfer size reduced
   - Log: compression ratio

### Edge Cases
9. Large files:
   - File > 100MB
   - Verify: transfer completes
   - Log: large file handling

10. Many files:
    - Project with > 10000 files
    - Verify: sync completes within timeout
    - Log: file count, sync time

11. Symlinks:
    - Project contains symlinks
    - Verify: handled correctly (follow or preserve)
    - Log: symlink handling mode

12. Special characters:
    - Files with spaces, unicode names
    - Verify: correctly synced
    - Log: special char handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All files sync correctly to real worker
- [ ] Exclusions work (target/, .git/objects/, etc)
- [ ] Incremental sync is efficient
- [ ] Large projects sync within timeout
- [ ] No data corruption
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:32.533703690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:54.017622702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:29:54.017375016Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1f2v (Project Sync Tests).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-1f2v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-2a7t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9ygg","depends_on_id":"bd-2bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:26:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
